learning_outcomes.description: Analyse common ethical dilemmas facing employees and managers in the contemporary business environment, both individually and as part of a team.
Demonstrate a comprehensive awareness and understanding of major business ethics theories, paradigms and approaches.
Analyse the factors that influence employee and managerial ethical decision-making.
Demonstrate knowledge of the practical challenges of managing ethical dilemmas and deviance in the workplace.
Critically reflect on your own values and behaviours and your role as an agent of positive ethical change.

assessments.due_date:
Case Study 1 16/03/2026

Case Study 2 30/03/2026

Case Study 3 13/04/2026

Case Study 4 20/04/2026

Case Study 5 27/04/2026

The assessments will occur in class during the following weeks: Week 4, 6, 7, 8 and 9.
assessments.task_description:
Each seminar topic will have a case (and additional material, as required) assigned as a required reading. Students will need to complete all required readings prior to class and attend the seminar to learn additional content (e.g., concepts, theories, models).

This task will also involve critiquing an AI-generated case study analysis, which will only be provided in class.

Students are only permitted to use the approved materials (provided at Blackboard and/or in class) to answer the questions.

During the allocated time in class, students will receive specific questions and will need to discuss and formulate their answers. Each team must submit their responses within the allocated time. Students will also need to provide hand-written notes taken during their discussions in class.

Team Formation

Preliminary teams will be formed in class in Weeks 1 and 2 and finalised in Week 4. Students will only be allowed to change teams in exceptional circumstances and with written permission of the course coordinator.

Marking

Each case study response will be marked out of 5. The marking rubric will be provided at Blackboard.

Peer Assessment: Buddycheck will be used to evaluate team members' individual contribution to the assessment task. If team members indicate that the other members generally contributed equally, all team members will receive the same mark. If team members indicate that one or more team members contributed less than others, these students' marks will be adjusted to reflect their average peer-rated percentage contribution to the workshop.

Note: Students must attend class during the first four weeks of the semester to join a team. Week 4 is the final opportunity to join a team. Students who do not attend class and do not provide an adequate reason for their absence accompanied by documentation (e.g., medical certificate) will not be allocated to a team and will receive 0 for the team component of this assessment task.

AI Statement:

This assessment task is to be completed in-person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.submission_guidelines: The answers must be submitted in paper along with handwritten notes from the discussions in class.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
assessments.late_submission: Answers must be submitted in class at the specified time. Late responses will not be marked.
assessments.additional_info: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.

assessments.due_date:
Week 11 - Week 13

The assessments will occur in class during the following weeks: Weeks 11, 12 and 13.
assessments.task_description:
In this task, you and your team will be required to analyse one contemporary ethical issue facing organisations today. Teams will be allocated topics in Week 5. If you wish to explore a different topic, you will need to seek approval from the course coordinator.

Following this, you will need to develop a workshop to teach the class about the ethical issue. The workshop must be engaging, highly interactive, and facilitators must not read from a script. Further information including the marking rubric will be provided at Blackboard. Workshops will be approximately 25 minutes in length.

Peer Assessment: Buddycheck will be used to evaluate team members' individual contribution to the assessment task. If team members indicate that the other members generally contributed equally, all team members will receive the same mark. If team members indicate that one or more team members contributed less than others, these students' marks will be adjusted to reflect their average peer-rated percentage contribution to the workshop.

Please Note: The assessment will be recorded for marking purposes per UQ Policy.

AI Statement:

Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) are emerging tools that may support students in completing this assessment task. Students may appropriately use AI and/or MT in completing this assessment task. Students must clearly reference any use of AI or MT in each instance.

A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Teams will be required to submit their PowerPoint slides and any additional materials one week prior to their scheduled workshop presentation date. These must be submitted at Blackboard otherwise the workshop cannot be marked.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.

assessments.task_description:
The final examination will consist of MCQs (Multiple Choice Questions) and short answer questions. It will cover major concepts and theories in the course, and questions may be drawn from all course content, including textbook chapters, seminar slides and discussions, case studies, and any other required activities/readings. More information will be provided in seminars and on the course Blackboard site

AI Statement

This assessment task is to be completed in-person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.submission_guidelines: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to defer this exam.

learning_activities.topic:
Introduction to Business Ethics

In this seminar we will discuss the structure and content of the course and the learning objectives. We will also consider the nature of ethics; approaches to ethical decision-making; and define business ethics. We will also further consider the importance of identifying one's personal values and how to create an individual ethical code.

Prior to class, you will need to complete the required readings at Blackboard and Ferrell et al. Chapter 1 and Chapter 7.


Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L05
learning_activities.topic_description: Introduction to Business Ethics In this seminar we will discuss the structure and content of the course and the learning objectives. We will also consider the nature of ethics; approaches to ethical decision-making; and define business ethics. We will also further consider the importance of identifying one's personal values and how to create an individual ethical code. Prior to class, you will need to complete the required readings at Blackboard and Ferrell et al. Chapter 1 and Chapter 7.

learning_activities.topic:
Stakeholder Relationships, Social Responsibility, and Corporate Governance

In this seminar we will discuss the importance of stakeholders and professional ethics and students will have the opportunity to form teams for the case-based assessments.

We will also discuss the process of case analysis that will be used in the course.

Prior to class, you will need to complete the required readings at Blackboard and Ferrell et al. Chapters 2 and 4.


Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03

learning_activities.topic:
Emerging Business Ethics Issues

In this seminar we will discuss major business ethics issues today, with a focus on unethical business practices.

Prior to class, you will need to complete the required readings at Blackboard and Ferrell et al. Chapter 5.

This week is the final opportunity to join a team for the case study response assessment task.

Assessment: Teams must submit their case study response in class.

learning_activities.topic:
Organisational Factors: The Role of Ethical Culture and Relationships.

In this seminar we will discuss the role of organisational factors in ethical decision-making, with a specific focus on building ethical cultures and the role of situational factors at work.

Prior to class, you will need to complete the required readings at Blackboard and Ferrell et al. Chapter 8 and Chapter 9.

Assessment: Teams must submit their case study response in class.
